revision.comment: 1 revision imported: taking a bunch of templates
Text replacement - "[[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]" to "<nowiki>[[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]</nowiki>"
Bot: convert local/ILL link → enwiki interwiki
Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
Bot: wrap 3 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23168229401|commit triggered pipeline]]

[[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Circa%2Fdoc here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{COinS safe|n|addtext=
The CS1 and CS2 templates have limited [[Help:Citation_Style_1#Date_format_compliance_with_Wikipedia's_Manual_of_Style|support]] for c. as an abbreviation in the date fields.}} {{Lua|Module:Check for unknown parameters}} {{Template shortcut|c.}} ==Usage== This template generates an abbreviation for ''[[wikt:circa#English|circa]]'': {{circa}} By default the abbreviation is marked with an HTML <abbr> tag, which in most browsers renders as a [[:en:Tooltip|Tooltip]] when hovered. ===Examples=== {| class="wikitable" |- ! Syntax ! What it looks like ! Notes |- | {{circa|1325}} | {{circa|1325}} | rowspan="2" | Default style: no line break allowed; thin space |- | {{circa|1325 BCE}} | {{circa|1325 BCE}} |- | {{circa|1325|lk=yes}} | {{circa|1325|lk=yes}} | Replace abbreviation tooltip with a link to Wiktionary instead |- | {{circa|1325|lk=no}} | {{circa|1325|lk=no}} | No abbreviation tooltip at all |- | {{circa|1000|1050}} | {{circa|1000|1050}} | For a range with both dates uncertain. No tooltip on the second instance of "c." |- | {{circa|1000–1050}} | {{circa|1000–1050}} | Another option for a range; but, per [[MOS:CIRCA]], prefer {{circa|1000|1050}} |} ===Parameters=== Using the date itself as parameter (for example, {{circa|1956}}, giving "{{circa|1956}}"), web browsers do not insert a line break anywhere in the string. By contrast, {{circa}} 1956 allows a line break between the "{{circa}}" and the number. Also, with the date as a parameter a thinner space is used with many browsers: * "{{circa|1956}}" is generated by {{circa|1956}}. * "{{circa}} 1956" is generated by {{circa}} 1956. If the parameter {{para|lk}} (for "link") is set as {{para|lk|no}} or {{para|lk|off}}, the abbreviation is rendered as "{{circa|lk=no}}", without a wikilink or abbreviation element. If it is absent, or set to {{para|lk|abbr}}, the abbreviation is rendered with the HTML <abbr> element. If it is set to {{para|lk|yes}} or {{para|lk|on}}, it is rendered as "{{circa|lk=yes}}", with a wikilink to the ''[[:wikt:circa]]'' article in Wiktionary and not an abbreviation element. lk=no can be used to avoid [[WP:Overlinking|overlinking]] when this template is used repeatedly in the body of an article. For example: * [[Dionysius Exiguus]] was born {{circa|470}}, lived in Rome from {{circa|lk=no|500}}, and died {{circa|lk=no|544}}. *:→ [[Dionysius Exiguus]] was born {{circa|470}}, lived in Rome from {{circa|lk=no|500}}, and died {{circa|lk=no|544}}. {{circa/doc/sortable}} ==Tracking categories== *{{clc|Pages using circa with unknown parameters}} ==See also== *{{tl|cx}}{{snd}}a shortcut with {{para|lk|no}} *{{tl|Born in}} *{{tl|Died in}} *{{tl|Floruit}} *{{tl|Married in}} *{{tl|Reign}} *[[:en:Tempore|Tempore]] ==TemplateData== {{templatedata header}} { "params": { "1": { "label": "year", "type": "string", "suggested": true, "description": "An approximate date", "example": "1965" }, "2": { "label": "end year", "type": "string", "description": "Use when the start and end dates of a range are both approximate.", "example": "1980" }, "sortable": { "label": "sortable", "description": "Set to “yes” for values in sortable tables", "suggestedvalues": [ "yes" ], "default": "yes" }, "lk": { "label": "link", "description": "If ‘on’, a link is added to the wiktionary article for circa. If ‘off’, the c. does not link or tooltip “circa”. Should be set to ‘off’ for the second and subsequent uses of this template. (Values ‘yes’ or ‘no’ may also be used.)", "type": "string", "suggestedvalues": [ "on", "off" ] } }, "paramOrder": [ "1", "2", "lk", "sortable" ], "description": "This template generates an abbreviation for circa." } {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Time and date abbreviation templates]] [[Category:Templates that add a tracking category]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
